intraday.summary: Put/call ratio of 1.58 with $95.8M in put premium vs $60.5M calls signals immediate selling pressure. Tech outperformance likely to reverse as Iran war headlines accelerate into US close
intraday.key_driver: Heavy institutional put buying and geopolitical risk escalation

next_day.summary: Iran war premium pricing in with oil shock fears. Rate hike expectations rising per EU officials. Private credit stress spreading. Overnight risk-off likely to dominate
next_day.key_driver: Geopolitical escalation forcing central banks to reconsider rate cuts

long_term.summary: Massive TSLA March puts ($647k), deep ITM META/MSFT puts signal institutional de-risking. Stagflation scenario building with war-driven oil shock meeting tight labor markets
long_term.key_driver: Stagflation forcing Fed pivot away from cuts toward potential hikes

news_impact: Iran war headlines dominating with oil reserve releases being considered by G7. Rate hike expectations returning as war-inflation fears mount. Private credit stress at JPMorgan adds systemic risk layer
options_flow_impact: Extreme bearish positioning with 1.58 put/call ratio. Massive TSLA March $337.50 puts and deep ITM protective puts on META/MSFT signal institutional exodus from growth names
risks: Fed emergency liquidity injection on credit stress
Strategic oil reserve release larger than expected

Large call buying provides near-term support but macro headwinds will overwhelm. High multiple names compress in stagflation
